Alright, I'm inside of involve.me and I want to show you a few things here. I want to show you first an example of our automated intimacy application on the back end and I'll call out a few kind of optimization tips that might be useful and then I'm going to show you a second example of this which actually has branch logic. So interesting thing to call out here when we launched automated intimacy we really wanted to have as many conversations with our audience as possible and we had the time to do it or at least we perceived we did. So we didn't actually put any kind of block and tackle or branching logic to hold applications or move people out of the funnel in place. We just we captured the answers to those questions and we knew that information but if someone still wanted to book the call even if they had said no to one of our transparency alerts in this case we still allowed that. So that's what you'll see in this kind of simplified example of our automated intimacy application. In the other example I'll show you it has branch logic in place which can allow you to move people out of the funnel if they aren't a good fit based on those transparency funnel. So you'll see it two ways and I'll show you a few other little things along the way. So again there are this is not the only tool for this this is just the one that I enjoy using. There are so many tools we could never show you them all so if you have questions around your tool bring those to a call if you're part of CTC Circle but you know how they most work is like this where you have your different types of elements you can add to the page so you can see where we've added the various questions that are part of this application to the flow and it does a really beautiful job of letting you design it. And what I really want to show you here at the end there is a essentially a thank you page and here what we're doing is we are setting a custom redirect you'll see here there's this pencil instead of taking them to a separate thank you page within involve.me we're sending them to a custom URL so that is that scheduling page on our website and this is a nice little optimisation I wanted to call out here if you're using QoT other scheduling tools may have this as well but one of the things that people find particularly annoying sometimes is if you ask them to fill out the same information more than one time in the same flow so in our application there is a step actually let me go back right here where we ask them for their name their phone number and their email before they continue and then what we're doing is we're sending them over to this scheduling page now if I just grab this link and let's just open up a different page here oops let's just pop that pop that in here so if I just go to this page directly and I click to schedule you'll see it's gonna ask me for my first last name but an email which is great but if you're going through this application flow and on the previous step we've already asked for that information someone might find it sort of annoying if you were to ask them for it again so how do we solve that in this redirect there are what's called URL parameters and these are quite simple acuity uses this language right here so basically after your URL wherever you have the acuity schedule embedded you just put question mark email and then again all of these different tools may have a different kind of language for this but in involve.me you just you do two of these squiggly brackets and email and that's how you pre-fill the information same thing here for first name it's and this is case sensitive meaning you know lower and upper need to be exactly as shown here you can pre-fill first name as well as last name following this structure and also the phone number so what this does is by adding these on to the URL so these are what they call answer piping variable variables because we've captured these answers in the previous step by adding those into the URL this makes it so when someone goes through this process it will actually pre-fill these values so you probably saw this or if you didn't catch it go back and watch kind of the fireside chat demo where we go through the flow and you'll see that that information is seamlessly entered into those fields so seems like a little thing and in a sense it is but it does great for a better experience and you know that translates over into every step of our engagement so that's a great little call out there the other thing I wanted to show you within this example is if we go to the settings there's a few things one we kept this very simple we just wanted to get a notification directly from this tool with the answers so we were optimizing for speed we didn't have ton of volume we don't have a big team so we chose not to in this case do some of the things that I'm going to show you where you move all this information directly into active campaign now there are benefits to that even for small teams so it's something we'll get to but this is kind of you know one of those things where you have to prioritize what's most important at any given point so just because you can do something doesn't mean you should do it it's really going to be specific to your process so in this case I wanted to show you that we were just getting a simple email when someone filled out the application it was going to both of us and that is how we would get the answers to those questions prior to the enrollment calls and then the only other thing I will show you here is if we go to edit the project and we look at that project here this copy of automated intimacy if we click this share and embed button here this is where we get that embed code so this is what I showed you in the Convertory and WordPress examples I think it was the WordPress example you just can find by going here to standard you can even kind of customize this a little bit but you're just going to go here get this embed code and that's exactly what you drop onto the page in your landing page builder so it's quite simple as that you can test everything right within involve.me but then when you're ready to deploy that application you'll grab this embed code to paste into your actual page so that is the first example I wanted to show you the second is where we have branch logic so I'm going to go to this branch logic demo that I created so what I did here was just for simplicity I just grabbed two of our transparency alerts that were actually part of our AI demo but I said you know what let's say that we fast forwarded and we have a thousand people that are very very much interested in scheduling a appointment or joining this program well Ryan and I as a team right now you know maybe you know and maybe even if we had a sales team because we need to be careful with our financial resources we need to be good steward in that we're not going to let people on to our appointment schedule if they are clearly not a good fit because they've kind of self-selected out based on those transparency alerts so using branch logic that's how we solve for this so example one you know we basically say hey we want self-sufficient entrepreneurs and business owners who don't have an urgent life or death problem are you with us on that yep or not right so if I look at this what I'm doing is I'm doing a logic jump so there's click behavior here and so I'm basically saying if they say yep meaning they're still with us we're not going to map that outcome we're just going to jump them to page two which is the next question but if they say nah or no we're going to send them to the outcome closing time and they're going to jump to that page which is finished that's going to just move them right out of the application no matter how many more questions are there we're saying you know this question is so important that they say yes to it they said no we're going to fast path them out of this application let's save them some time entering more questions let's save us some time having a call right but if they click yes then we will move them to number two and then we're going to ask another question hey investments four to 15k are you still with me so same sort of thing except answering just continues to the next page and this is because this is the last one those pages are the outcomes as the let's do this or the closing so if they said sure thing we map them to the outcome let's do this and if they said whoa no thanks we again send them to closing time and so what you have is multiple outcomes you can have more than two actually but if you look at this all this is is a page title just so I know what it is and then a custom URL redirect so I just you know popped in automated intimacy.com but in reality what we would do here is we would send them to the schedule right so that's where where if they answered the yes to all the transparencies we move them to the scheduling page if they said no to any of them in this case what I'm doing is I'm sending them to a different URL I'm sending them to our closing time page now you could vary there's a few things you could do here you this is just a silly closing time page but you could send them to a specific downsell page you could send them to just a thank you page it says we're reviewing your application and we will be back in touch with you shortly which in that case they wouldn't even necessarily know that they were going to be given the opportunity to book a call they just think you're processing all the applications so I've done that in cases where perhaps the transparency alerts don't always give me the full picture and I really want to see how thoughtfully they replied to the open-ended questions I won't immediately exclude them but I want to review those before I offer them an appointment and so they just think that well it's the truth we're reviewing their application and if we decide after reviewing that that they're a good fit we will send out a we'll send a link to the scheduler manually and if we determine that it doesn't seem like a good fit well of course we're still going to follow up with them with whatever the best guidance and advice that we have with them given the stage of where they are at so that's essentially how branch lodging logic works there's many tools again for this this is just one but this will give you an idea of the concept and if we go and actually run this you can see it in you can see it in action so I'm going to view this so you can see here I'm going to say yes which is going to move us to the question to sure thing and because I chose yes to both of those it's going to send us to the booking page now if I go back let's go all the way to the beginning here and let's hit no on the first question you'll see it's going to immediately pop us out and take us to closing time and one more demo here if I hit yes on the first question and then well no no thanks on the second same thing it's going to take us up to closing time so that's an example how you can use branch logic and yeah that's all I got
